principles.name: Fairness
principles.definition: To design AI systems that avoid biasness or discrimination against the users.
principles.description: AI systems must not discriminate based on age, gender, religion, or ethnicity. Training data must be diverse, representative, and free from biases. Encompasses values of justice, respect for diversity, inclusion, equality, and avenues for redress.
keyElements: Non-discrimination across protected characteristics (age, gender, religion, ethnicity)
Diverse and representative training data free from biases
Bias-mitigation techniques in model development
Continuous monitoring of algorithm performance for unintentional biases
Ethical checks on AI output for fairness and impartiality
Avenues for redress when unfair outcomes occur

stakeholderObligations.developers: Ensure diverse training data, test models against diverse datasets, adopt bias-mitigation techniques
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Adopt ethical checks to ensure AI output is fair and unbiased
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Formulate policies that promote fairness and equal treatment in AI systems

principles.name: Reliability, Safety and Control
principles.definition: To ensure that AI systems are safe and secure, and perform as intended.
principles.description: AI systems must operate reliably and consistently under both normal and unexpected conditions. Systems must provide proper access control and protection in critical situations, with human control mechanisms for high-risk applications.
keyElements: Reliable and consistent operation under normal and unexpected conditions
Proper access control and protection in critical situations
Rigorous testing and validation of algorithm accuracy
Resilience against unauthorized access and compromise
Human control mechanisms for high-risk applications
Comprehensive validation protocols before deployment

stakeholderObligations.developers: Conduct rigorous testing, validate algorithms, implement access controls, deploy validation protocols
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Report unexpected AI behaviour, maintain awareness of system limitations
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Establish safety standards and testing requirements for AI systems

principles.name: Privacy and Security
principles.definition: To test AI systems regularly to ensure safety and reliability of the same.
principles.description: AI systems must undergo rigorous testing and risk assessments to confirm they secure personal data. Systems must safeguard personal data and adhere to international privacy standards with protection against unauthorized data access.
keyElements: Rigorous testing and risk assessments for data security
Safeguarding personal data per international privacy standards
Protection mechanisms against unauthorized data access
Compliance with established data protection frameworks
Regular testing cycles for ongoing safety and reliability

stakeholderObligations.developers: Implement data protection measures, conduct regular security testing, comply with privacy frameworks
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Exercise data rights, report privacy concerns
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Enforce data protection laws and ensure AI compliance with privacy standards

principles.name: Inclusiveness
principles.definition: To foster inclusivity and equal access to AI advancements.
principles.description: AI systems must be accessible and beneficial to all societal segments. Solutions must consider diverse user needs without creating disparities, preventing unequal access that could exacerbate existing social divides.
keyElements: Accessible and beneficial AI for all societal segments
Diverse user needs considered in system design
Prevention of disparities in AI access and benefits
Equitable participation across demographic groups
Special consideration for vulnerable communities
AI advancements must not deepen existing inequalities

stakeholderObligations.developers: Design systems accessible to diverse user groups, avoid one-size-fits-all approaches
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Promote equitable access to AI benefits across all communities

principles.name: Transparency
principles.definition: To promote transparency in AI algorithms for stakeholders to evaluate risks of AI, especially in the situation where AI is being used in decision-making processes.
principles.description: Full disclosure of AI use in decision-making and its intended purpose. Training data, biases, quality verification procedures, and maintenance protocols must be documented and available to stakeholders.
keyElements: Full disclosure of AI use in decision-making and its purpose
Information on training data and embedded biases
Documented data quality verification procedures
Available maintenance and assessment protocols
Mechanisms for users to challenge AI-driven decisions
Understandable algorithms for stakeholder evaluation

stakeholderObligations.developers: Disclose AI purpose, document training data, provide explainable algorithms
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Exercise right to challenge AI-driven decisions
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Mandate transparency requirements for AI decision-making systems

principles.name: Accountability
principles.definition: To hold AI developers, owners and actors accountable for the proper functioning of AI systems.
principles.description: Developers, owners, and operators are accountable for system performance and compliance with AI governance standards and ethical principles. Four accountability elements: system purpose, technology capability, quality and reliability, and consideration of sensitive users.
keyElements: Accountability for system performance and compliance
Four elements: purpose, capability, quality/reliability, sensitive users
Clear responsibility assignment for harmful AI outcomes
Policymaker responsibility for AI-related regulations
End user rights: disclosure, objection, deletion, collective redress

stakeholderObligations.developers: Accept accountability for system function, address all four accountability elements
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Exercise rights to information disclosure, data objection, deletion, and collective redress
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Formulate and implement AI-related regulations across sectors

principles.name: Pursuit of Human Benefit and Happiness
principles.definition: To ensure AI technologies are human-centric and respect fundamental rights.
principles.description: AI systems must respect human-centred values and enhance quality of life. Systems must promote happiness while allowing human intervention to preserve autonomy. Technology must serve humanity's broader interests.
keyElements: Respect for human-centred values and fundamental rights
Enhancement of quality of life and well-being
Human intervention preserved to maintain autonomy
AI serving humanity's broader interests
Prevention of harm or misuse of AI technology
Human decision-making authority maintained

stakeholderObligations.developers: Design AI to enhance well-being, maintain human oversight, prevent misuse
stakeholderObligations.endUsers: Maintain awareness of AI impact on personal well-being
stakeholderObligations.policymakers: Ensure AI policies prioritise human benefit and societal progress
